result:
Both bugs fixed. Final build green.

## Report

**Root cause:** Two related bugs in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs`, both in const-variable assignment detection:

1. **`check_readonly`** (line ~2125) emitted `"attempt to assign to const variable"` without the variable name. The test `local gab = string.format("attempt to assign to const variable '%s'", name); assert(... string.find(msg, gab))` searches for the name-bearing form, so all `checkro` calls (locals.lua lines 189–193, 195, 200) failed.

2. **`new_upvalue`** (line ~2240) had two `TODO(port)` lines that hardcoded `up.kind = VarKind::Reg.as_u8()`. When a `<const>` local was captured by a closure, the upvalue lost its const-kind, so `check_readonly` (UpVal arm) couldn't detect assignment to `z = 32` inside `function foo()` (locals.lua lines 195, 200).

**Fix:**
- Format the variable name into the error message via `String::from_utf8_lossy(vname.as_bytes())`.
- In `new_upvalue`, look up the original kind from `fs.prev`: for `ExprKind::Local` use `get_local_var_desc(ls, prev, v.u.var_vidx)`; for `ExprKind::UpVal` use `prev.f.upvalues[v.u.info].kind`. Mirrors `newupvalue` in `lparser.c:172`.

**New state:** Both checkro cases now return `nil, "attempt to assign to const variable 'y'"` / `... 'z'` as Lua expects. Did not re-run full `locals.lua` (per instructions); mega-loop will re-test.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/checkro_test.lua << 'EOF'
local function checkro (name, code)
    local st, msg = load(code)
    print("st=", st, "msg=", msg)
end
checkro("y", "local x, y <const>, z = 10, 20, 30; x = 11; y = 12")
checkro("z", [[
    local a, z <const>, b = 10;
    function foo() a = 20; z = 32; end
]])
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs /tmp/checkro_test.lua 2>&1 | tail -20
